posts.author: RupeeRiser
posts.text: BSE replacing Wipro in Nifty 50 — I think it is got all to do with Thursday being the preferred day for FII’s to trade Weekly Expiring Derivatives in India. ( Being that the BSE Sensex Options expiry was swapped with Nifty50 Weeklies to Expire on Thursdays) Not sure what the reason is. Some Thursdays, I still get the feeling that some legacy Market Making Algorithms have still not realized that Nifty50 Weekly Expires were shifted to Tuesdays.

posts.author: Akash_Shah
posts.text: BSE replacing Wipro in Nifty 50 — Typically Nifty Add/removes are done in March and September. So even if this has to happen, I doubt it will happen before September. At this point, looks like rumour to jack up price further.

posts.author: Akash_Shah
posts.text: BSE replacing Wipro in Nifty 50 — It is rare but possible. Criteria is based on free float market cap but it also looks at average data over past 6 months or so. On top index is reviewed only once in 6 month. So that is like a year of gap. So normally a rising stock would enter NN50 first and then N50, but in rare situation, where stock has a strong bull run in short period (like BSE did), chances are that during last review it didn’t qualify for N100 and when next review comes it has already surpassed NN50 threshold and might be in N50 range already.

posts.author: neha1101
posts.text: BSE replacing Wipro in Nifty 50 — This is great news. Today, I was looking at Icici pru nifty financials ex bank ETF and what caught my attention was BSE having 8.81% weights. My question is was BSE a Nifty next 50 stock? I do not think so as I am invested in Nifty next 50s stocks. How come, bse can jump the 50 stocks and get into Nifty 50? I always thought any stock which comes into Nifty 50 should come from Nifty next 50 and so on… So kind of surprising, this thing BSE jumping and getting into Nifty 50

posts.author: Sankalp
posts.text: BSE replacing Wipro in Nifty 50 — BSE may replace Wipro in Nifty 50. Estimated passive flows: • BSE inflows: ~$657M • Wipro outflows: ~$225M Pretty remarkable turnaround for BSE honestly. From being seen as the weaker exchange for years to now potentially entering Nifty 50 with massive institutional inflows. IMG_4458 1179×1283 179 KB
